name: Wren Allowyn
shortDescription: Circus tumbler turned rogue, running from a contract she walked out on.
description: Came up through the Circus of the Moon as a tumbler, the kind they put on a rope between two opera-house balconies because the crowd liked her. "Wren Allowyn" was the stage name — small body, a name with an elven flourish that played well from a balcony — and the only one she answers to now; the one before is in a Circus ledger she has not seen in two years. Trained for the other work too, the way every Circus performer is, and made it as far as the dressing-room door on the night of her first contract before she walked out. The Circus does not chase a runner immediately; it waits, in the way only the Circus knows how, until the runner is somewhere where being found will hurt the most. She wears a small painted moon medallion under her shirt that she should have given back. She has not, and she will not, and she knows what that means.
